prompt: What was Nixon referring to when he said, "I am not a crook"?
system: Answer the following question concisely and truthfully. If you are not sure, say so.
response: When Richard Nixon said, "I am not a crook," he was referring to allegations of wrongdoing related to the Watergate scandal and his administration's involvement in the cover-up of the break-in at the Democratic National Committee headquarters. He made this statement during a press conference on November 17, 1973, in response to accusations of corruption and misconduct.
